content.problemStatement: Write a program that takes the two different strings as input and prints them in a single line separated by spaces.
constraints: Each string can contain any alphanumeric characters and spaces.
The length of each string is less than or equal to 100 characters.
The input strings are guaranteed to be different.
content.inputFormat: First line contains a string. Second line contains another string.
content.outputFormat: Print the two input strings separated by a space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Two strings are read and printed separated by a space.
Another example of reading two strings and printing them separated by a space.
Strings can contain leading or trailing spaces.

solution.approach: Read two strings from the input, then concatenate them with a space in between and print the result.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n), where n is the total length of the two input strings, because we are reading and printing the strings.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n), where n is the total length of the two input strings, because we are storing the strings in memory.

code.Python3:
str1 = input()
str2 = input()
print(str1, str2)
